chief_complaint: A 54-year-old female presented with recurrent episodes of altered sensorium lasting 4-6 minutes with snoring and unresponsiveness, and confusion during resolution.
history: The patient had recurrent hyponatremia and hypoglycemia. Electroencephalogram (EEG) showed epileptiform discharges. The recurrent seizure episodes persisted in spite of antiepileptic drugs. Serum autoimmune encephalitis panel was positive for leucine-rich glioma-inactivated 1 (LGI1) antibody.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Electroencephalogram (EEG): epileptiform discharges. Serum autoimmune encephalitis panel: positive for leucine-rich glioma-inactivated 1 (LGI1) antibody (2+) (titer 1:10). Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) examination: glucose 174.7 mg/dL, protein 60.4 mg/dL, total leukocyte count (TLC) 5, acid-fast bacilli (AFB), Gram stain-no organism, culture sensitivity sterile, Cryptococcus antigen, cobwebbing absent, BioFire CSF meningitis/encephalitis panel was negative.
differentials: Syndrome of Inappropriate Antidiuretic Hormone Secretion (SIADH)
correct_ranking: Syndrome of Inappropriate Antidiuretic Hormone Secretion (SIADH)
